states_detail.upload-file-selected: covered (a11y focus-return sub-requirement broken, see fix_list)

accessibility_blocking: upload-file-selected: 'Remove {fileName}' click handler calls dropzoneRef.current?.focus() synchronously in the same tick that onDocumentRemove() is invoked. The dropzone <div> is conditionally unmounted while document.status is 'selected'/'success', so dropzoneRef.current is null until the parent re-renders with the new status. Focus is therefore never actually returned to the dropzone after removal, violating the spec.json a11y requirement for 'upload-file-selected' ('...returns focus to the dropzone after removal'). The existing test only asserts onDocumentRemove was called, not that focus moved, so the regression is untested and unnoticed.
accessibility_minor: upload-empty: dropzone is implemented as a <div role="button" tabIndex={0}> with a manual onKeyDown handler rather than 'a real button/label' as literally specified in spec.json's accessibility bullet ('native <input type="file"> triggered via a real button/label, not a bare div onClick'). It is functionally keyboard-operable (Enter/Space wired, proper role/tabIndex), but does not match the letter of the spec's preferred implementation pattern.

focus: the 'Focus' story renders the default personal-info step with no interaction/pseudo-class applied, so it does not visually demonstrate the :focus-visible ring described by the state — it is not distinguishable from the 'Empty' or 'StepPersonalInfo' stories.

upload-drag-active test quality: the test queries `getByRole('status', { name: '' })` (an unnamed status region) rather than asserting on the live-region's content directly by a stable query, which is fragile but not itself an a11y defect in the component.
fix_list: In KycVerificationWizard.tsx, replace the synchronous `dropzoneRef.current?.focus()` call inside handleRemoveFile with a useEffect that watches `documentState.status` and calls `dropzoneRef.current?.focus()` only after the status has transitioned away from 'selected'/'success' (mirroring the existing heading-focus-on-step-change pattern), so focus is returned to the dropzone once it is actually back in the DOM.

Add/extend the 'upload-file-selected' test to assert that after clicking the remove button and the parent re-renders document status to 'empty', the dropzone (getByRole('button', { name: /drag and drop or browse/i })) actually receives focus — not just that onDocumentRemove was called — to prevent this regression from being reintroduced silently.

(Minor) Consider swapping the dropzone's <div role="button"> for a native <button> (or a <label> wrapping the visually-hidden <input type="file">) to align literally with the spec.json accessibility bullet preferring a 'real button/label' over a div with manual ARIA semantics.

(Minor) Update the 'Focus' story to demonstrate the focus-visible affordance explicitly (e.g. via Storybook's play function to tab/focus an element) rather than rendering an unfocused default state indistinguishable from 'Empty'.
